title: Handle symlinks when checking DB vs runtime configs
body:
When Podman starts, it checks a number of critical runtime paths against stored values in the database to make sure that existing containers are not broken by a configuration change. We recently made some changes to this logic to make our handling of the some options more sane (StaticDir in particular was set based on other passed options in a way that was not particularly sane) which has made the logic more sensitive to paths with symlinks. As a simple fix, handle symlinks properly in our DB vs runtime comparisons.

The BoltDB bits are uglier because very, very old Podman versions sometimes did not stuff a proper value in the database and instead used the empty string. SQLite is new enough that we don't have to worry about such things.

Fixes #20872

If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:

```release-note
Fixed a bug where Podman would not run any command on systems with a symlinked $HOME
```

This Debian build failure is very interesting. Will investigate on Tuesday.

Ugh, the [cockpit integration tests](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/8af4e664-4415-4a8c-b0c9-c91edbc63175/) don't get very far, early setup failure:
```
+ podman pull quay.io/prometheus/busybox
Error: database volume path "" does not match our volume path ".": database configuration mismatch
```

That's the same error as the debian-13 build that you already mentioned.

I think that's a regression from this PR. E.g. #20879 passed, and our [podman-next nightly COPR run](https://github.com/cockpit-project/cockpit-podman/issues/1495) [faied too](https://cockpit-logs.us-east-1.linodeobjects.com/pull-0-20231202-015044-39de27b4-fedora-39-podman-next/log.html), but on a criu regression (I'll report this on Monday).

Repushed with what I think is a fix. It seems like there are cases where paths in the config can be set to "" - I'm assuming that means that they'll be set to default in c/storage? There was already a workaround for this case in the BoltDB code which I assumed was cruft from a long-fixed bug, but evidently not.
OK, that seems to have done it.

Instead of keeping file paths, would it make sense to keep a different stable identifier?  Hmm, actually since the runtime config is always transient, couldn't the runtime config have e.g. the (device, inode) pair of the db directory?

> Instead of keeping file paths, would it make sense to keep a different stable identifier? Hmm, actually since the runtime config is always transient, couldn't the runtime config have e.g. the (device, inode) pair of the db directory?

The db config is not transient, it would then cause issues for users who backup and restore the directory at some point in the future as the inode and or device might change.
